frameworks.name: compositional Bayesian regression with Gaussian process kernel grammar
frameworks.one_line: complex functions decomposed into simpler kernel building blocks via grammar

frameworks.name: non-compositional structure learning
frameworks.one_line: alternative function-learning accounts without grammar-based decomposition

core_question: Do people learn complex functions by compositionally decomposing them into simpler building blocks?
key_assumptions: function learning can be formalized as Bayesian regression
Gaussian process kernels can be composed via a grammar
inductive biases are recoverable from human extrapolation and interpolation choices
manipulations: compositional vs non-compositional functions in extrapolation/interpolation tasks
predictability, memorability, and numerosity judgments of functions
key_findings: participants preferred compositional over non-compositional extrapolations and interpolations
prior-elicitation revealed inductive bias for compositional structure
compositional functions judged more predictable, more memorable, and lower in perceived numerosity
interpretations: human intuitive theory of functions is inherently compositional
compositionality is a domain-general inductive bias supporting function learning
